prompt: A Brazilian user emailed us requesting deletion of all their personal data. We store their data in our CRM, email platform, and analytics tool. How do we handle this under LGPD?
assertions: Grounds the deletion right in Art. 18 (item V — anonymization, blocking or deletion of unnecessary/excessive/noncompliant data; item VI — deletion of data processed based on consent) rather than inventing rights
Includes identity verification of the requester before acting on the request
Covers deletion across all three named systems (CRM, email platform, analytics tool) plus backups, and notifying processors/sub-processors so the deletion is replicated

States lawful retention exceptions under Art. 16 (compliance with legal/regulatory obligation such as tax and transaction records, research with anonymization where possible, transfer per legal requirements, or exclusive non-shared controller use) and advises telling the user what was retained and why

Gives a response timeframe consistent with LGPD/ANPD practice (15 days is the Art. 19 reference for the full access report; deletion 'without undue delay') and does NOT invent a statutory 24/48/72-hour deletion deadline; advises logging the request and outcome for accountability
